tEn: An Empirical Model of Large-Batch Training
tZh: 大规模批量训练的实证模型
sEn: A simple statistic called the gradient noise scale predicts the largest useful batch size across many domains.
sZh: 一个称为梯度噪声尺度的简单统计量可以预测多个领域中最大有用的批量大小。
absEn: In an increasing number of domains it has been demonstrated that deep learning models can be trained using relatively large batch sizes without sacrificing data efficiency. However the limits of this massive data parallelism seem to differ from domain to domain, ranging from batches of tens of thousands in ImageNet to batches of millions in RL agents that play the game Dota 2. To our knowledge there is limited conceptual understanding of why these limits to batch size differ or how we might choose the correct batch size in a new domain. In this paper, we demonstrate that a simple and easy-to-measure statistic called the gradient noise scale predicts the largest useful batch size across many domains and applications, including a number of supervised learning datasets (MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR-10, ImageNet, Billion Word), reinforcement learning domains (Atari and Dota), and even generative model training (autoencoders on SVHN). We find that the noise scale increases as the loss decreases over a training run and depends on the model size primarily through improved model performance. Our empirically-motivated theory also describes the tradeoff between compute-efficiency and time-efficiency, and provides a rough model of the benefits of adaptive batch-size training.
absZh: 越来越多的领域表明，深度学习模型可以使用相对较大的批量大小进行训练，而不会牺牲数据效率。然而，这种大规模数据并行的限制似乎因领域而异，从ImageNet的数万批量到Dota 2游戏智能体的数百万批量。据我们所知，对于这些批量大小限制为何不同，或者如何在新领域中选择正确的批量大小，目前缺乏概念上的理解。在本文中，我们证明了一个简单且易于测量的统计量——梯度噪声尺度——可以预测许多领域和应用中最大有用的批量大小，包括多个监督学习数据集（MNIST、SVHN、CIFAR-10、ImageNet、Billion Word）、强化学习领域（Atari和Dota），甚至生成模型训练（SVHN上的自编码器）。我们发现，随着训练过程中损失的降低，噪声尺度会增加，并且模型大小主要通过改进模型性能来影响噪声尺度。我们的经验驱动理论还描述了计算效率和时间效率之间的权衡，并提供了自适应批量大小训练益处的粗略模型。

contrib.en: Proposes gradient noise scale as a simple statistic to predict the largest useful batch size across diverse domains.
contrib.zh: 提出梯度噪声尺度作为简单统计量，用于预测跨不同领域的最大有用批量大小。

contrib.en: Derives a theoretical framework linking noise scale to the compute-time tradeoff curve in large-batch training.
contrib.zh: 推导出将噪声尺度与大批量训练中计算-时间权衡曲线联系起来的理论框架。

contrib.en: Empirically validates predictions across 8 tasks including supervised learning, RL, and generative models.
contrib.zh: 在包括监督学习、强化学习和生成模型的8个任务上实证验证了预测。

contrib.en: Shows noise scale increases during training and is larger for complex tasks, with weak dependence on model size.
contrib.zh: 表明噪声尺度在训练过程中增加，复杂任务中更大，且与模型大小弱相关。

contrib.en: Provides a practical method to measure noise scale with minimal overhead in data-parallel training.
contrib.zh: 提供了一种在数据并行训练中以最小开销测量噪声尺度的实用方法。

contrib.en: Demonstrates efficiency gains from adaptive batch-size training based on noise scale.
contrib.zh: 展示了基于噪声尺度的自适应批量大小训练的效率提升。

limits.en: Theory relies on local quadratic approximation and may not capture global optimization dynamics.
limits.zh: 理论依赖于局部二次近似，可能无法捕捉全局优化动态。

limits.en: Noise scale prediction accuracy varies across tasks; ratio to critical batch size can differ by an order of magnitude.
limits.zh: 噪声尺度预测精度因任务而异；与临界批量大小的比率可能相差一个数量级。

limits.en: Assumes optimal learning rate tuning; noise scale depends on learning rate via training temperature.
limits.zh: 假设学习率调优最优；噪声尺度通过训练温度依赖于学习率。

limits.en: Does not address generalization gap; focuses solely on training loss optimization.
limits.zh: 未解决泛化差距问题；仅关注训练损失优化。

limits.en: Empirical validation limited to specific architectures and optimizers; may not generalize to all settings.
limits.zh: 实证验证限于特定架构和优化器；可能无法推广到所有设置。

items.en: In an increasing number of domains it has been demonstrated that deep learning models can be trained using relatively large batch sizes without sacrificing data efficiency. However the limits of this massive data parallelism seem to differ from domain to domain, ranging from batches of tens of thousands in ImageNet to batches of millions in RL agents that play the game Dota 2. To our knowledge there is limited conceptual understanding of why these limits to batch size differ or how we might choose the correct batch size in a new domain. In this paper, we demonstrate that a simple and easy-to-measure statistic called the gradient noise scale predicts the largest useful batch size across many domains and applications, including a number of supervised learning datasets (MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR-10, ImageNet, Billion Word), reinforcement learning domains (Atari and Dota), and even generative model training (autoencoders on SVHN). We find that the noise scale increases as the loss decreases over a training run and depends on the model size primarily through improved model performance. Our empirically-motivated theory also describes the tradeoff between compute-efficiency and time-efficiency, and provides a rough model of the benefits of adaptive batch-size training.
items.zh: 在越来越多的领域中，研究表明深度学习模型可以使用相对较大的批量大小进行训练，而不会牺牲数据效率。然而，这种大规模数据并行的限制似乎因领域而异，从ImageNet的数万批量到玩Dota 2的强化学习智能体的数百万批量。据我们所知，对于这些批量大小限制为何不同，或者如何在新领域中选择正确的批量大小，目前缺乏概念上的理解。在本文中，我们证明了一个简单且易于测量的统计量——梯度噪声尺度——能够预测许多领域和应用中最大的有用批量大小，包括多个监督学习数据集（MNIST、SVHN、CIFAR-10、ImageNet、Billion Word）、强化学习领域（Atari和Dota），甚至生成模型训练（SVHN上的自编码器）。我们发现，随着训练过程中损失的降低，噪声尺度会增加，并且主要通过与模型性能提升相关的模型大小来影响。我们基于经验动机的理论还描述了计算效率与时间效率之间的权衡，并提供了自适应批量大小训练收益的粗略模型。

items.en: The last few years have seen a rapid increase in the amount of computation used to train deep learning models [AH18]. A major enabler as well as a limiting factor in this growth has been parallelism – the extent to which a training process can be usefully spread across multiple devices. Regardless of how much total computation is available, if model training cannot be sufficiently parallelized, then it may take too much serial time and therefore may be practically infeasible.
items.zh: 过去几年，用于训练深度学习模型的计算量迅速增加[AH18]。这种增长的一个主要推动因素和限制因素是并行性——即训练过程可以在多大程度上有效地分布在多个设备上。无论总计算量有多少，如果模型训练无法充分并行化，那么它可能需要过多的串行时间，从而在实践中不可行。

items.en: A very common source of parallelism in deep learning has been data parallelism, which involves splitting a batch of data across multiple devices and then aggregating and applying the resulting gradients. Data parallelism requires fast communication between devices, but also requires that large batches are algorithmically effective in accelerating learning. Recently, a number of papers have shown empirically that on specific datasets or tasks, large batch sizes can achieve almost linear speed-ups in training without substantially harming sample efficiency or generalization. For example, batch sizes of 8 thousand [GDG+17], 16 thousand [SKYL17], 32 thousand [YGG17, YZH+17, ASF17], and even 64 thousand [JSH+18] examples have been effectively employed to train ImageNet, and batch sizes of thousands have been effective for language models and generative models [OEGA18, PKYC18, BDS18]. This phenomenon is not confined to supervised learning: in reinforcement learning, batch sizes of over a million timesteps (with tens of thousands of environments running in parallel) have been used in a Dota-playing agent [BCD+18], and even in simple Atari environments batch sizes of several thousand timesteps have proved effective [AAG+18, HQB+18, SA18]. These discoveries have allowed massive amounts of data and computation to be productively poured into models in a reasonable amount of time, enabling more powerful models in supervised learning, RL, and other domains.
items.zh: 深度学习中一个非常常见的并行化来源是数据并行，它涉及将一批数据分割到多个设备上，然后聚合并应用得到的梯度。数据并行需要设备之间的快速通信，但也要求大批量在加速学习方面具有算法上的有效性。最近，许多论文在特定数据集或任务上经验性地表明，大批量大小可以在训练中实现几乎线性的加速，而不会显著损害样本效率或泛化能力。例如，8000[GDG+17]、16000[SKYL17]、32000[YGG17、YZH+17、ASF17]甚至64000[JSH+18]个样本的批量大小已被有效用于训练ImageNet，而数千的批量大小已用于语言模型和生成模型[OEGA18、PKYC18、BDS18]。这种现象不仅限于监督学习：在强化学习中，超过一百万个时间步的批量大小（并行运行数万个环境）已被用于Dota游戏智能体[BCD+18]，甚至在简单的Atari环境中，数千个时间步的批量大小也被证明是有效的[AAG+18、HQB+18、SA18]。这些发现使得大量数据和计算能够在合理的时间内高效地注入模型，从而在监督学习、强化学习和其他领域实现更强大的模型。

items.en: However, for a given dataset and model, there is little to guide us in predicting how large a batch size we can feasibly use, why that number takes a particular value, or how we would expect it to differ if we used a different dataset or model. For example, why can we apparently use a batch size of over a million when training a Dota agent, but only thousands or tens of thousands when training an image recognition model? In practice researchers tend to simply experiment with batch sizes and see what works, but a downside of this is that large batch sizes often require careful tuning to be effective (for example, they may require a warmup period or an unusual learning rate schedule), so the fact that it is possible to use a large batch size can remain undiscovered for a long time. For example, both the Atari and ImageNet tasks were for several years conventionally run with a substantially smaller batch size than is now understood to be possible. Knowing ahead of time what batch size we expect to be effective would be a significant practical advantage in training new models.
items.zh: 然而，对于给定的数据集和模型，几乎没有指导我们预测可以实际使用多大的批量大小，为什么该数字取特定值，或者如果我们使用不同的数据集或模型，它会如何变化。例如，为什么在训练Dota智能体时我们可以使用超过一百万的批量大小，而在训练图像识别模型时只能使用数千或数万？在实践中，研究人员倾向于简单地通过实验来尝试批量大小并观察效果，但这有一个缺点：大批量大小通常需要仔细调整才能有效（例如，它们可能需要预热期或不寻常的学习率调度），因此使用大批量大小的可能性可能长期未被发现。例如，Atari和ImageNet任务在传统上多年来都使用比现在已知可能更小的批量大小。提前知道我们期望有效的批量大小将在训练新模型时具有重要的实际优势。

items.en: In this paper we attempt to answer some of these questions. We measure a simple empirical statistic, the gradient noise scale111Similar metrics have appeared previously in the literature. We discuss related work in Section 4. (essentially a measure of the signal-to-noise ratio of gradient across training examples), and show that it can approximately predict the largest efficient batch size for a wide range of tasks. Our model also predicts a specific shape for the compute/time tradeoff curve, illustrated in Figure 1. Our contributions are a mix of fairly elementary theory and extensive empirical testing of that theory.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们试图回答其中一些问题。我们测量了一个简单的经验统计量——梯度噪声尺度（本质上是对梯度在训练样本间信噪比的度量），并表明它可以近似预测各种任务的最大有效批量大小。我们的模型还预测了计算/时间权衡曲线的特定形状，如图1所示。我们的贡献包括相当基础的理论和该理论的广泛经验测试。

items.capEn: Figure 1: The tradeoff between time and compute resources spent to train a model to a given level of performance takes the form of a Pareto frontier (left). Training time and compute cost are primarily determined by the number of optimization steps and the number of training examples processed, respectively. We can train a model more quickly at the cost of using more compute resources. On the right we show a concrete example of the Pareto frontiers obtained from training a model to solve the Atari Breakout game to different levels of performance. The cost and training time depend on the computing architecture and are shown approximately.
items.capZh: 图1：将模型训练到给定性能水平所花费的时间与计算资源之间的权衡呈现为帕累托前沿（左）。训练时间和计算成本主要由优化步骤数和处理的训练样本数决定。我们可以以使用更多计算资源为代价更快地训练模型。右侧展示了训练模型解决Atari Breakout游戏到不同性能水平所获得的帕累托前沿的具体示例。成本和训练时间取决于计算架构，并近似显示。

items.en: On the conceptual side, we derive a framework which predicts, under some basic assumptions, that training should parallelize almost linearly up to a batch size equal to the noise scale, after which there should be a smooth but relatively rapid switch to a regime where further parallelism provides minimal benefits. Additionally, we expect that the noise scale should increase during training as models get more accurate, and should be larger for more complex tasks, but should not have a strong dependence on model size per se. We also provide an analysis of the efficiency gains to be expected from dynamically adjusting the batch size according to noise scale during training. Finally, we predict that, all else equal, the noise scale will tend to be larger in complex RL tasks due to the stochasticity of the environment and the additional variance introduced by the credit assignment problem.
items.zh: 在概念方面，我们推导了一个框架，该框架在基本假设下预测，训练应该几乎线性地并行化，直到批量大小等于噪声尺度，之后应该平滑但相对快速地过渡到进一步并行化收益最小的区域。此外，我们预计噪声尺度在训练过程中会随着模型变得更准确而增加，并且对于更复杂的任务应该更大，但不应与模型大小本身有强依赖关系。我们还分析了在训练过程中根据噪声尺度动态调整批量大小所预期的效率提升。最后，我们预测，在其他条件相同的情况下，由于环境的随机性和信用分配问题引入的额外方差，复杂强化学习任务中的噪声尺度往往更大。

items.en: On the empirical side, we verify these predictions across 8 tasks in supervised learning, RL, and generative models, including ImageNet, CIFAR-10, SVHN, MNIST, BillionWord, Atari, OpenAI’s Dota agent [BCD+18], and a variational autoencoder for images. For each of these tasks we demonstrate that the noise scale accurately predicts the largest usable batch size (at the order of magnitude level) and that gains to parallelism degrade in the manner predicted by theory. We also show that the noise scale increases over the course of training and demonstrate efficiency gains from dynamic batch size tuning. The noise scale eventually becomes larger for more performant models, but this appears to be caused by the fact that more performant models simply achieve a better loss.
items.zh: 在经验方面，我们在监督学习、强化学习和生成模型的8个任务中验证了这些预测，包括ImageNet、CIFAR-10、SVHN、MNIST、BillionWord、Atari、OpenAI的Dota智能体[BCD+18]以及图像变分自编码器。对于每个任务，我们证明了噪声尺度能够准确预测最大可用批量大小（在数量级水平上），并且并行化的收益以理论预测的方式退化。我们还展示了噪声尺度在训练过程中增加，并展示了动态批量大小调整的效率提升。噪声尺度最终对于性能更好的模型变得更大，但这似乎是因为性能更好的模型只是实现了更好的损失。

items.en: The rest of this paper is organized as follows. In Section 2, we derive a simple conceptual picture of the noise scale, data parallelism, and batch sizes, and explain what it predicts about optimal batch sizes and how they vary over the course of training and across tasks. We build on this analysis to study training efficiency in Section 2.3. Then in Section 3 we empirically test the predictions in Section 2 and explore how the noise scale varies with dataset, model size, and learning paradigm (supervised learning vs RL vs generative models). Section 4 describes related work and Section 5 discusses the implications of these results and possible future experiments.
items.zh: 本文的其余部分组织如下。在第2节中，我们推导了噪声尺度、数据并行和批量大小的简单概念图景，并解释了它对最优批量大小以及它们在训练过程中和跨任务如何变化的预测。我们在第2.3节中基于此分析研究了训练效率。然后在第3节中，我们经验性地测试了第2节中的预测，并探讨了噪声尺度如何随数据集、模型大小和学习范式（监督学习与强化学习与生成模型）变化。第4节描述了相关工作，第5节讨论了这些结果的含义和可能的未来实验。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Less noisy gradient estimates allow SGD-type optimizers to take larger steps, leading to convergence in a smaller number of iterations. As an illustration, we show two optimization trajectories using momentum in a quadratic loss, with different step sizes and different amounts of artificial noise added to the gradient.
items.capZh: 图2：噪声较小的梯度估计允许SGD型优化器采取更大的步长，从而在更少的迭代次数内收敛。作为说明，我们展示了在二次损失中使用动量的两个优化轨迹，具有不同的步长和添加到梯度中的不同人工噪声量。

items.en: Before working through the details of the gradient noise scale and the batch size, it is useful to present the intuitive picture. Suppose we have a function we wish to optimize via stochastic gradient descent (SGD). There is some underlying true optimization landscape, corresponding to the loss over the entire dataset (or, more abstractly, the loss over the distribution it is drawn from). When we perform an SGD update with a finite batch size, we’re approximating the gradient to this true loss. How should we decide what batch size to use?
items.zh: 在详细讨论梯度噪声尺度和批量大小之前，先呈现直观图景是有用的。假设我们有一个希望通过随机梯度下降（SGD）优化的函数。存在一个潜在的真实优化景观，对应于整个数据集上的损失（或者更抽象地说，对应于其抽取分布的损失）。当我们使用有限批量大小执行SGD更新时，我们是在近似这个真实损失的梯度。我们应该如何决定使用多大的批量大小？

items.en: When the batch size is very small, the approximation will have very high variance, and the resulting gradient update will be mostly noise. Applying a bunch of these SGD updates successively will average out the variance and push us overall in the right direction, but the individual updates to the parameters won’t be very helpful, and we could have done almost as well by aggregating these updates in parallel and applying them all at once (in other words, by using a larger batch size). For an illustrative comparison between large and small batch training, see Figure 2.
items.zh: 当批量大小非常小时，近似将具有非常高的方差，得到的梯度更新主要是噪声。连续应用这些SGD更新将平均掉方差并推动我们整体朝着正确的方向前进，但参数的单个更新不会非常有帮助，我们几乎可以通过并行聚合这些更新并一次性应用它们（即使用更大的批量大小）来获得相同效果。关于大批量和小批量训练的说明性比较，见图2。

items.en: By contrast, when the batch size is very large, the batch gradient will almost exactly match the true gradient, and correspondingly two randomly sampled batches will have almost the same gradient. As a result, doubling the batch size will barely improve the update – we will use twice as much computation for little gain.
items.zh: 相比之下，当批量大小非常大时，批量梯度将几乎完全匹配真实梯度，相应地，两个随机采样的批次将具有几乎相同的梯度。因此，将批量大小加倍几乎不会改善更新——我们将使用两倍的计算量而收益甚微。

items.en: Intuitively, the transition between the first regime (where increasing the batch size leads to almost perfectly linear speedups) and the second regime (where increasing the batch size mostly wastes computation) should occur roughly where the noise and signal of the gradient are balanced – where the variance of the gradient is at the same scale as the gradient itself222Note that these considerations are completely agnostic about the size of the dataset itself.. Formalizing this heuristic observation leads to the noise scale.
items.zh: 直观上，从第一个区域（增加批量大小导致几乎完全线性的加速）到第二个区域（增加批量大小主要浪费计算）的过渡应该大致发生在梯度的噪声和信号平衡的地方——即梯度的方差与梯度本身处于同一尺度。形式化这一启发式观察引出了噪声尺度。

items.en: The situation is shown pictorially in Figure 1. For a given model, we’d like to train it in as little wall time as possible (x-axis) while also using as little total computation as possible (y-axis) – this is the usual goal of parallelization. Changing the batch size moves us along a tradeoff curve between the two. Initially, we can increase the batch size without much increase in total computation, then there is a “turning point” where there is a substantive tradeoff between the two, and finally when the batch size is large we cannot make further gains in training time. In the conceptual and experimental results below, we formalize these concepts and show that the bend in the curve (and thus the approximate largest effective batch size) is in fact set roughly by the noise scale.
items.zh: 这种情况在图1中以图形方式展示。对于给定的模型，我们希望以尽可能少的挂钟时间（x轴）进行训练，同时使用尽可能少的总计算量（y轴）——这是并行化的通常目标。改变批量大小使我们沿着两者之间的权衡曲线移动。最初，我们可以增加批量大小而不会大幅增加总计算量，然后有一个“转折点”，两者之间存在实质性权衡，最后当批量大小很大时，我们无法在训练时间上取得进一步进展。在下面的概念和实验结果中，我们形式化了这些概念，并表明曲线弯曲处（从而近似最大的有效批量大小）实际上大致由噪声尺度设定。

full.sec: 2.2 Gradients, Batches, and the Gradient Noise Scale
full.secZh: 2.2 梯度、批次与梯度噪声尺度

items.en: We’ll now formalize the intuitions described in Section 2.1. Consider a model, parameterized by variables θ∈ℝD𝜃superscriptℝ𝐷\theta\in\mathbb{R}^{D}, whose performance is assessed by a loss function L​(θ)𝐿𝜃L\left(\theta\right). The loss function is given by an average over a distribution ρ​(x)𝜌𝑥\rho\left(x\right) over data points x𝑥x. Each data point x𝑥x has an associated loss function Lx​(θ)subscript𝐿𝑥𝜃L_{x}\left(\theta\right), and the full loss is given by L​(θ)=𝔼x∼ρ​[Lx​(θ)]𝐿𝜃subscript𝔼similar-to𝑥𝜌delimited-[]subscript𝐿𝑥𝜃L\left(\theta\right)=\mathbb{E}_{x\sim\rho}\left[L_{x}\left(\theta\right)\right]333In the context of reinforcement learning, the loss could be the surrogate policy gradient loss, and the distribution ρ𝜌\rho would be nonstationary..
items.zh: 现在我们将形式化第2.1节中描述的直觉。考虑一个模型，由变量θ∈ℝD参数化，其性能通过损失函数L(θ)评估。损失函数由数据点x上的分布ρ(x)的平均值给出。每个数据点x都有一个相关的损失函数Lx(θ)，总损失由L(θ)=𝔼x∼ρ[Lx(θ)]给出。

items.en: We would like to minimize L​(θ)𝐿𝜃L\left(\theta\right) using an SGD-like optimizer, so the relevant quantity is the gradient G​(θ)=∇L​(θ)𝐺𝜃∇𝐿𝜃G\left(\theta\right)=\nabla L\left(\theta\right). However, optimizing L​(θ)𝐿𝜃L\left(\theta\right) directly would be wasteful if not impossible, since it would require processing the entire data distribution every optimization step. Instead, we obtain an estimate of the gradient by averaging over a collection of samples from ρ𝜌\rho, called a batch:
items.zh: 我们希望使用类似SGD的优化器最小化L(θ)，因此相关量是梯度G(θ)=∇L(θ)。然而，直接优化L(θ)将是浪费甚至不可能的，因为它需要在每个优化步骤处理整个数据分布。相反，我们通过平均来自ρ的样本集合（称为一个批次）来获得梯度的估计：

items.en: This approximation forms the basis for stochastic optimization methods such as mini-batch stochastic gradient descent (SGD) and Adam [KB14]. The gradient is now a random variable whose expected value (averaged over random batches) is given by the true gradient. Its variance scales inversely with the batch size B𝐵B444This is strictly true only when training examples are sampled independently from the same data distribution. For example, when batches are sampled without replacement from a dataset of size D𝐷D, the variance instead scales like (1B−1D)1𝐵1𝐷\left(\frac{1}{B}-\frac{1}{D}\right). For simplicity, we restrict ourself to the case where B≪Dmuch-less-than𝐵𝐷B\ll D or where batches are sampled with replacement, but our conclusions can be altered straightforwardly to account for correlated samples.:
items.zh: 这种近似构成了随机优化方法的基础，如小批量随机梯度下降（SGD）和Adam[KB14]。梯度现在是一个随机变量，其期望值（在随机批次上平均）由真实梯度给出。其方差与批量大小B成反比缩放：

items.en: where the per-example covariance matrix is defined by
items.zh: 其中每个样本的协方差矩阵定义为：

items.en: The key point here is that the minibatch gradient gives a noisy estimate of the true gradient, and that larger batches give higher quality estimates. We are interested in how useful the gradient is for optimization purposes as a function of B𝐵B, and how that might guide us in choosing a good B𝐵B. We can do this by connecting the noise in the gradient to the maximum improvement in true loss that we can expect from a single gradient update. To start, let G𝐺G denote the true gradient and H𝐻H the true Hessian at parameter values θ𝜃\theta. If we perturb the parameters θ𝜃\theta by some vector V𝑉V to θ−ϵ​V𝜃italic-ϵ𝑉\theta-\epsilon V, where ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon is the step size, we can expand true loss at this new point to quadratic order in ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon:
items.zh: 关键点在于，小批量梯度给出了真实梯度的有噪声估计，并且更大的批次提供更高质量的估计。我们感兴趣的是梯度对于优化目的的有用性如何随B变化，以及这如何指导我们选择好的B。我们可以通过将梯度中的噪声与我们从单个梯度更新中期望的真实损失的最大改进联系起来。首先，令G表示真实梯度，H表示参数值θ处的真实Hessian矩阵。如果我们用某个向量V将参数θ扰动为θ-ϵV，其中ϵ是步长，我们可以将新点处的真实损失展开到ϵ的二次阶：

items.capEn: Figure 3: Larger batch sizes yield estimated gradients that are closer to the true gradient, on average. Larger step sizes can be used when the estimated gradient is closer to the true gradient, so more progress can be made per step. Left: A large step size used with a small batch size can lead to instability, as illustrated for a quadratic loss. Right: Equation 2.6 predicts that the ‘turning point’ after which larger batch sizes become less helpful is the noise scale ℬℬ\mathcal{B}, where the training speed drops to 50% of the maximum possible.
items.capZh: 图3：更大的批次大小平均而言能产生更接近真实梯度的估计梯度。当估计梯度更接近真实梯度时，可以使用更大的步长，因此每一步可以取得更多进展。左图：在二次损失中，小批次大小使用大步长可能导致不稳定性。右图：方程2.6预测，批次大小超过噪声尺度ℬ后，增大批次大小的帮助会减少，此时训练速度降至最大可能速度的50%。

items.en: If we had access to the noiseless true gradient G𝐺G and used it to perturb the parameters, then Equation 2.4 with V=G𝑉𝐺V=G would be minimized by setting ϵ=ϵmax≡|G|2GT​H​Gitalic-ϵsubscriptitalic-ϵmaxsuperscript𝐺2superscript𝐺𝑇𝐻𝐺\epsilon=\epsilon_{\mathrm{max}}\equiv\frac{|G|^{2}}{G^{T}HG}. However, in reality we have access only to the noisy estimated gradient Gestsubscript𝐺estG_{{\rm est}} from a batch of size B𝐵B, thus the best we can do is minimize the expectation 𝔼​[L​(θ−ϵ​Gest)]𝔼delimited-[]𝐿𝜃italic-ϵsubscript𝐺est\mathbb{E}[L\left(\theta-\epsilon G_{{\rm est}}\right)] with respect to ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon. This expected value can be evaluated using Equation 2.2:
items.zh: 如果我们能获得无噪声的真实梯度G并用它来扰动参数，那么令V=G的方程2.4将通过设置ϵ=ϵmax≡|G|2/(GTHG)来最小化。然而，实际上我们只能从大小为B的批次中获得带噪声的估计梯度Gest，因此我们最多只能通过最小化关于ϵ的期望E[L(θ-ϵGest)]来优化。该期望值可利用方程2.2计算：

items.en: Minimizing this equation with respect to ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon leads to:
items.zh: 关于ϵ最小化该方程得到：

items.en: as the optimal step size, which produces an optimal improvement in the loss from the noisy gradient:
items.zh: 作为最优步长，它从带噪声梯度中产生最优的损失改进：

items.en: Above, we have defined the noise scale as:
items.zh: 上面，我们将噪声尺度定义为：

items.en: Note that our definition of the noise scale is independent of the size of the full training set. If we use a step size larger than twice ϵoptsubscriptitalic-ϵopt\epsilon_{{\rm opt}}, the loss may increase, leading to divergence, as illustrated in Figure 3.
items.zh: 注意，我们对噪声尺度的定义与完整训练集的大小无关。如果使用的步长大于2ϵopt的两倍，损失可能会增加，导致发散，如图3所示。

items.en: Despite the many unfounded assumptions in the above derivation, we will find that equations 2.7 and 2.8 provide a helpful guide to the behavior of large-batch training, even when using other optimizers (including momentum, Adam, and RMSProp).
items.zh: 尽管上述推导中有许多未经证实的假设，但我们会发现方程2.7和2.8为大批次训练的行为提供了有用的指导，即使在使用其他优化器（包括动量、Adam和RMSProp）时也是如此。

items.en: For a discussion of the dependence of the noise scale on the learning rate, see Appendix C on the ‘temperature’ of training.
items.zh: 关于噪声尺度对学习率依赖性的讨论，请参见附录C关于训练“温度”的内容。

items.en: Equation 2.7 implies that when the batch size is much smaller than the noise scale, B≪ℬnoisemuch-less-than𝐵subscriptℬnoiseB\ll\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}}, the second term in the denominator dominates the first, so increasing the batch size B𝐵B linearly increases the progress in loss. This is the small batch regime, where increases in batch size linearly speed up training. By contrast, when B≫ℬnoisemuch-greater-than𝐵subscriptℬnoiseB\gg\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}}, then the first term dominates, so that increasing B𝐵B has almost no effect on the progress in loss. This is the large batch regime where increases in batch size do not speed up training and simply waste computation; the switch between the two occurs at B≈ℬnoise𝐵subscriptℬnoiseB\approx\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}} (see Figure 3).
items.zh: 方程2.7表明，当批次大小远小于噪声尺度时，即B≪ℬnoise，分母中的第二项占主导，因此增大批次大小B会线性增加损失进展。这是小批次区域，批次大小的增加线性加速训练。相反，当B≫ℬnoise时，第一项占主导，增大B对损失进展几乎没有影响。这是大批次区域，增大批次大小不会加速训练，只会浪费计算；两者之间的切换发生在B≈ℬnoise处（见图3）。

items.en: The noise scale in Equation 2.8 requires some overhead to compute due to the presence of the Hessian H𝐻H. We can estimate it by measuring Δ​Lopt​(B)Δsubscript𝐿opt𝐵\Delta L_{{\rm opt}}\left(B\right) using a series of line searches in the direction of a gradient measured with various batch sizes B𝐵B and fitting the result to Equation 2.7. This allows us to estimate ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}} as well as to empirically test whether Equation 2.7 actually fits the data (we discuss these local tests more in Section 3).
items.zh: 方程2.8中的噪声尺度由于涉及Hessian矩阵H，需要一些额外计算来估计。我们可以通过使用不同批次大小B测量梯度方向上的线搜索来估计ΔLopt(B)，并将结果拟合到方程2.7，从而估计ℬnoise，并实证检验方程2.7是否拟合数据（我们在第3节中进一步讨论这些局部测试）。

items.en: The situation gets even simpler if we make the (unrealistic) assumption that the optimization is perfectly well-conditioned – that the Hessian is a multiple of the identity matrix. If that is the case, then Equation 2.8 reduces to:
items.zh: 如果我们做出（不现实的）假设，即优化是完全良态的——Hessian是单位矩阵的倍数，那么情况会更简单。如果是这样，方程2.8简化为：

items.en: which says that the noise scale is equal to the sum of the variances of the individual gradient components, divided by the global norm of the gradient555One might also use preconditioned gradients, obtained for example by dividing gradient components by the square root of the Adam optimizer’s [KB14] accumulated variances. We experimented with this but found mixed results. – essentially a measure of how large the gradient is compared to its variance. It is also a measure of the scale at which the estimated and true gradient become close in L2superscript𝐿2L^{2} space (having non-trivial dot product) – the expected normalized L2superscript𝐿2L^{2} distance is given by:
items.zh: 这表明噪声尺度等于各个梯度分量的方差之和除以梯度的全局范数——本质上衡量了梯度相对于其方差的大小。它也是估计梯度与真实梯度在L2空间中变得接近（具有非平凡点积）的尺度——期望的归一化L2距离由下式给出：

items.en: In practice, we find that ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} and ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}} typically differ only by a small constant multiplicative factor, particularly when we employ common training schemes that improve conditioning. In our empirical work we will sometimes compute ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}}, but will primarily compute ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} instead, as it requires less computational expense. In Appendix A.1, we provide an extremely simple method to measure this simplified noise scale with negligible overhead in the context of data-parallel training.
items.zh: 在实践中，我们发现ℬsimple和ℬnoise通常只相差一个小的常数乘法因子，特别是当我们采用改善条件的常见训练方案时。在我们的实证工作中，有时会计算ℬnoise，但主要计算ℬsimple，因为它需要的计算量较小。在附录A.1中，我们提供了一种极其简单的方法，在数据并行训练的背景下以可忽略的开销测量这个简化的噪声尺度。

full.secZh: 2.3 数据/时间效率权衡的预测

items.en: Thus far our analysis has only involved a single point in the loss landscape. But in Section 3 we will show that Equation 2.7 nevertheless predicts the dependence of training speed on batch size remarkably well, even for full training runs that range over many points in the loss landscape. By averaging Equation 2.7 over multiple optimization steps (see Appendix D), we find a simple relationship between training speed and data efficiency:
items.zh: 到目前为止，我们的分析只涉及损失景观中的一个点。但在第3节中，我们将展示方程2.7仍然能很好地预测训练速度对批次大小的依赖性，即使对于跨越损失景观中许多点的完整训练运行也是如此。通过对多个优化步骤平均方程2.7（见附录D），我们发现了训练速度与数据效率之间的简单关系：

items.en: Here, S𝑆S and Sminsubscript𝑆minS_{{\rm min}} represent the actual and minimum possible number of steps taken to reach a specified level of performance, respectively, and E𝐸E and Eminsubscript𝐸minE_{{\rm min}} represent the actual and minimum possible number of training examples processed to reach that same level of performance. Since we are training at fixed batch size666We discuss the benefits of dynamically varying the batch size in Appendix D, we have Etot=B​Stotsubscript𝐸tot𝐵subscript𝑆totE_{{\rm tot}}=BS_{{\rm tot}}. We define the critical batch size by an empirical fit to the above equation, as
items.zh: 这里，S和Smin分别表示达到指定性能水平所需的实际和最小可能步数，E和Emin分别表示达到相同性能水平所需的实际和最小可能训练样本数。由于我们以固定批次大小进行训练，有Etot=BStot。我们通过经验拟合上述方程定义临界批次大小为：

items.en: Our model predicts ℬcrit≈ℬnoisesubscriptℬcritsubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm crit}}\approx\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}}, where ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}} is appropriately averaged over training (see Appendix D). Note that the noise scale can vary significantly over the course of a training run, so the critical batch size also depends on the level of performance to which we train the model.
items.zh: 我们的模型预测ℬcrit≈ℬnoise，其中ℬnoise是在训练过程中适当平均的（见附录D）。注意，噪声尺度在训练过程中可能显著变化，因此临界批次大小也取决于我们训练模型达到的性能水平。

items.en: The resulting tradeoff curve in serial time vs total compute has a hyperbolic shape represented in Figure 1. The goal of optimization is to reach a given level of performance with minimal S𝑆S and E𝐸E – but as depicted in Figure 1, there are tradeoffs involved, as very small S𝑆S may require very large E𝐸E, and vice versa. When we choose B=ℬcrit𝐵subscriptℬcritB=\mathcal{B}_{{\rm crit}}, the two sides of Equation 2.11 are both 111, so that training takes twice as many passes through the training data as an optimally data-efficient (small-batch) run would take, and twice as many optimization steps as an optimally time-efficient (large-batch) run would take.
items.zh: 由此产生的串行时间与总计算量之间的权衡曲线具有双曲线形状，如图1所示。优化的目标是以最小的S和E达到给定的性能水平——但如图1所示，存在权衡，因为非常小的S可能需要非常大的E，反之亦然。当我们选择B=ℬcrit时，方程2.11的两边都等于1，因此训练所需的训练数据遍历次数是最优数据效率（小批次）运行的两倍，优化步数是最优时间效率（大批次）运行的两倍。

items.en: The mathematical argument in the previous sections depends on several assumptions and caveats, and it is useful to list these all in one place, in order to help clarify where and why we might expect the quantities in equations 2.8 and 2.9 to be relevant to training:
items.zh: 前面几节的数学论证依赖于几个假设和注意事项，有必要将它们列在一起，以帮助阐明我们预期方程2.8和2.9中的量在哪些方面以及为何与训练相关：

items.en: Short-horizon bias: The picture in Section 2.2 is a strictly local picture – it tells us how to best improve the loss on the next gradient step. Greedily choosing the best local improvement is generally not the best way to globally optimize the loss (see e.g. [WRLG18]). For example, greedy optimization might perform poorly in the presence of bad local minima or when the landscape is ill-conditioned. The critical batch size would then be reduced by the extent to which noise is beneficial.
items.zh: 短视偏差：第2.2节的描述是严格局部的——它告诉我们如何在下一步梯度中最好地改进损失。贪婪地选择最佳局部改进通常不是全局优化损失的最佳方式（例如参见[WRLG18]）。例如，在存在不良局部最小值或条件不良的景观中，贪婪优化可能表现不佳。临界批次大小会因噪声有益的程度而减小。

items.en: Poor conditioning: In poorly conditioned optimization problems, parameter values often oscillate along the large-curvature directions rather than decreasing in a predictable way (see e.g. [Goh17] and Appendix E.1). This means that Equation 2.7 will not perfectly reflect the amount of optimization progress made per step. Nevertheless, we will see that it still accurately predicts the relative speed of training at different batch sizes via the resulting tradeoff Equation 2.11.
items.zh: 条件不良：在条件不良的优化问题中，参数值通常沿大曲率方向振荡，而不是以可预测的方式减小（例如参见[Goh17]和附录E.1）。这意味着方程2.7不会完美反映每步的优化进展。然而，我们将看到，通过得到的权衡方程2.11，它仍然能准确预测不同批次大小下训练的相对速度。

items.en: Simplified noise scale: As noted in Section 2.2, whenever we use the simplified noise scale (Equation 2.9) rather than the exact noise scale (Equation 2.8), this number may be inaccurate to the extent that the Hessian is not well-conditioned. Different components of the gradient can have very different noise scales.
items.zh: 简化噪声尺度：如第2.2节所述，当我们使用简化噪声尺度（公式2.9）而非精确噪声尺度（公式2.8）时，若Hessian矩阵条件数不佳，该数值可能不准确。梯度的不同分量可能具有非常不同的噪声尺度。

items.en: Learning rate tuning: The arguments in Section 2.2 assume that we take the optimal step size and maximize the expected improvement in loss, Equation 2.6. In practice learning rates are unlikely to be perfectly tuned, so that the actual improvement in loss (and thus the scaling of training with batch size) may not perfectly reflect Equation 2.7. However, by trying to choose the best learning rate schedules (or by simply doing a grid search) we can reduce this source of error. In addition, the noise scale depends strongly on the learning rate via a ‘temperature’ of training, though this source of error is small as long as the learning rate is reasonably close to optimal. We provide a more detailed discussion of this dependence in Appendix C.
items.zh: 学习率调优：第2.2节的论证假设我们采用最优步长并最大化损失的期望改进（公式2.6）。在实践中，学习率不太可能完美调优，因此损失的实际改进（以及训练随批量大小的缩放）可能不完全符合公式2.7。然而，通过尝试选择最佳学习率调度（或简单地进行网格搜索），我们可以减少这一误差来源。此外，噪声尺度通过训练的“温度”强烈依赖于学习率，但只要学习率接近最优，这一误差来源就很小。我们在附录C中提供了关于这种依赖关系的更详细讨论。

items.en: Quadratic approximation: The Taylor expansion in Equation 2.4 is only to second order, so if third order terms are important, in either the distribution of gradient samples or the optimization landscape, then this may introduce deviations from our conceptual model, and in particular deviations from Equation 2.7. Intuitively, since parameter updates are local and often quite small we suspect that the previous two sources of error will be more important than this third one.
items.zh: 二次近似：公式2.4中的泰勒展开仅到二阶，因此如果三阶项在梯度样本分布或优化景观中很重要，则可能导致偏离我们的概念模型，特别是偏离公式2.7。直观上，由于参数更新是局部的且通常很小，我们怀疑前两个误差来源比第三个更重要。

items.en: Generalization: The picture in Section 2.2 says nothing about generalization – it is strictly about optimizing the training loss as a mathematical function. Some papers have reported a “generalization gap” in which large batch sizes lead to good training loss but cause a degradation in test loss, apparently unrelated to overfitting [KMN+16, HHS17]. The arguments in Section 2.2 don’t exclude this possibility, but recent work [SLA+18] has found no evidence of a generalization gap when hyperparameters are properly tuned.
items.zh: 泛化：第2.2节的描述未涉及泛化——它严格关于作为数学函数的训练损失优化。一些论文报告了“泛化差距”，即大批量导致良好的训练损失但造成测试损失下降，这似乎与过拟合无关[KMN+16, HHS17]。第2.2节的论证不排除这种可能性，但最近的工作[SLA+18]发现，当超参数适当调优时，没有泛化差距的证据。

items.en: Despite these potential issues in our conceptual model, we’ll show in Section 3 that the noise scale is overall a good empirical predictor of the critical batch size. Furthermore, we will see that most training runs fit Equation 2.11 remarkably well.
items.zh: 尽管我们的概念模型存在这些潜在问题，我们将在第3节展示，噪声尺度总体上是一个良好的经验预测指标，用于预测临界批量大小。此外，我们将看到大多数训练运行与公式2.11拟合得非常好。

full.sec: 2.5 Expected Patterns in the Noise Scale

items.en: In the next section we will measure the noise scale for a number of datasets and confirm its properties. However, it is worth laying out a few patterns we would expect it to exhibit on general grounds:
items.zh: 在下一节中，我们将测量多个数据集的噪声尺度并确认其性质。然而，值得列出我们预期它通常表现出的几种模式：

items.en: Larger for difficult tasks: We expect ℬℬ\mathcal{B} to be larger for more complex/difficult777To be clear, we do not expect this to be the primary difference between more and less difficult tasks. Other difficulty metrics such as the intrinsic dimensionality [LFLY18] appear to be unrelated to the amount of gradient noise, though it would be interesting if there were some connection. tasks, because individual data points will be less correlated, or only correlated in a more abstract way. This may apply both over the course of training on a given dataset (where we may pick the ‘low-hanging fruit’ first, leaving a long tail of more complicated things to learn) or in moving from easier to harder datasets and environments. In reinforcement learning, we expect environments with sparse rewards or long time horizons to have larger noise scale. We also expect generative models to have smaller ℬℬ{\cal B} as compared to classifiers training on the same dataset, as generative models may obtain more information from each example.
items.zh: 对于困难任务更大：我们预期ℬ对于更复杂/更困难的任务更大，因为单个数据点之间的相关性更弱，或者仅以更抽象的方式相关。这可能适用于给定数据集上的训练过程（我们可能先挑选“低垂的果实”，留下更复杂事物的长尾需要学习），或者从较简单数据集和环境转向更困难的数据集和环境。在强化学习中，我们预期稀疏奖励或长时间跨度的环境具有更大的噪声尺度。我们还预期生成模型与在同一数据集上训练的分类器相比具有更小的ℬ，因为生成模型可能从每个样本中获得更多信息。

items.en: Growth over training: ℬℬ\mathcal{B} will grow when the gradient decreases in magnitude, as long as the noise tr​(Σ)trΣ{\rm tr}(\Sigma) stays roughly constant. Since |G|𝐺|G| decreases as we approach the minimum of a smooth loss, we would expect ℬℬ\mathcal{B} to increase during neural network training.
items.zh: 随训练增长：只要噪声tr(Σ)大致保持恒定，当梯度幅度减小时，ℬ将增长。由于|G|在接近平滑损失最小值时减小，我们预期ℬ在神经网络训练期间会增加。

items.en: Weak dependence on model size: The number of model parameters in the neural network cancels in the noise scale, so we do not expect ℬℬ\mathcal{B} to exhibit a strong dependence on model size (at fixed loss). As discussed above, models that achieve better loss will tend to have a higher noise scale, and larger models often achieve better loss, so in practice we do expect larger models to have higher noise scale, but only through the mechanism of achieving better loss.
items.zh: 对模型大小的弱依赖性：神经网络中模型参数的数量在噪声尺度中抵消，因此我们不预期ℬ对模型大小（在固定损失下）表现出强依赖性。如上所述，实现更好损失的模型往往具有更高的噪声尺度，而更大的模型通常实现更好的损失，因此在实践中我们确实预期更大的模型具有更高的噪声尺度，但仅通过实现更好损失的机制。

items.en: Learning rate tuning: The noise scale will be artificially inflated if the learning rate is too small, due to the ‘temperature’ dependence described in Appendix C. To get a useful measurement of the noise scale, the learning rate needs to be appropriate to the current point in parameter space.
items.zh: 学习率调优：如果学习率太小，由于附录C中描述的“温度”依赖性，噪声尺度将被人为放大。为了获得噪声尺度的有用测量，学习率需要适合参数空间中的当前点。

items.en: The first and last points can be exhibited analytically in toy models (see Appendix C), but we do not expect theoretical analyses to provide a great deal of insight beyond the intuitions above. Instead, we will focus on confirming these expectations empirically.
items.zh: 第一点和最后一点可以在玩具模型中解析地展示（见附录C），但我们不期望理论分析能提供超出上述直觉的大量见解。相反，我们将专注于通过实验确认这些预期。

items.en: To summarize, our model makes the following predictions about large-batch training:
items.zh: 总结，我们的模型对大批量训练做出以下预测：

items.en: The tradeoff between the speed and efficiency of neural network training is controlled by the batch size and follows the form of Equation 2.11.
items.zh: 神经网络训练速度与效率之间的权衡由批量大小控制，并遵循公式2.11的形式。

items.en: The critical batch size ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} characterizing cost/time tradeoffs can be predicted at the order of magnitude level by measuring the gradient noise scale, most easily in the simplified form ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}} from Equation 2.9.
items.zh: 表征成本/时间权衡的临界批量大小ℬ_crit可以通过测量梯度噪声尺度在数量级水平上预测，最简单地使用公式2.9中的简化形式ℬ_simple。

items.en: The noise scale can vary significantly over the course of a training run, which suggests that the critical batch size also depends on the chosen level of model performance.
items.zh: 噪声尺度在训练过程中可能显著变化，这表明临界批量大小也取决于所选的模型性能水平。

items.en: The noise scale depends on the learning rate via the ‘temperature’ of training, but is consistent between well-tuned training runs (see Appendix C).
items.zh: 噪声尺度通过训练的“温度”依赖于学习率，但在调优良好的训练运行之间是一致的（见附录C）。

items.capEn: Figure 4: The “simple noise scale” roughly predicts the maximum useful batch size for many ML tasks. We define this “critical batch size” to be the point at which compute efficiency drops below 50% optimal, at which point training speed is also typically 50% of optimal. Batch sizes reported in number of images, tokens (for language models), or observations (for games). We show the critical batch size for a full training run, and the noise scale appropriately averaged over a training run (see Appendix D). Due to resource constraints, for Dota 5v5 we show the batch size used by the OpenAI Dota team as a lower bound for the critical batch size.
items.capZh: 图4：“简单噪声尺度”大致预测了许多机器学习任务的最大有用批量大小。我们将此“临界批量大小”定义为计算效率降至最优的50%以下的点，此时训练速度通常也是最优的50%。批量大小以图像数量、词元（对于语言模型）或观测数量（对于游戏）报告。我们展示了整个训练运行的临界批量大小，以及适当平均后的噪声尺度（见附录D）。由于资源限制，对于Dota 5v5，我们展示了OpenAI Dota团队使用的批量大小作为临界批量大小的下界。

items.en: We now test the predictions of Section 2 on a range of tasks, including image classification, language modeling, reinforcement learning, and generative modeling. The tasks range from very simple (MNIST) to very complex (5v5 Dota), which allows us to test our model’s predictions in drastically varying circumstances. Our central experimental test is to compare the prediction made by the gradient noise scale ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} for each task, to the actual limits of batch size ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} found by carefully tuned full training runs at an exhaustive range of batch sizes. The overall results of this comparison are summarized in Figure 4. We find that the gradient noise scale predicts the critical batch size at the order of magnitude level, even as the latter varies from 20 (for an SVHN autoencoder) to over 10 million (consistent with prior results reported in [BCD+18]). Details about the hyperparameters, network architectures, and batch size searches are described in Appendix A.4. Below we describe the individual tasks, the detailed measurements we perform on each task, and the results of these measurements.
items.zh: 我们现在在第2节的预测上测试一系列任务，包括图像分类、语言建模、强化学习和生成建模。任务范围从非常简单（MNIST）到非常复杂（5v5 Dota），这使我们能够在截然不同的情况下测试我们模型的预测。我们的核心实验测试是将每个任务的梯度噪声尺度ℬ_simple的预测与通过精心调优的完整训练运行在详尽批量大小范围内找到的实际批量大小限制ℬ_crit进行比较。该比较的总体结果总结在图4中。我们发现梯度噪声尺度在数量级水平上预测了临界批量大小，即使后者从20（对于SVHN自编码器）变化到超过1000万（与[BCD+18]中先前报告的结果一致）。关于超参数、网络架构和批量大小搜索的详细信息在附录A.4中描述。下面我们描述各个任务、我们对每个任务进行的详细测量以及这些测量的结果。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Left: The optimal learning rate is displayed for a range of batch sizes, for an SVHN classifier trained with SGD. The optimal learning rate initially scales linearly as we increase the batch size, leveling off in the way predicted by Equation 2.7. Right: For a range of batch sizes, we display the average loss progress Δ​L​(B)Δ𝐿𝐵\Delta L\left(B\right) that can be made from a batch of size B𝐵B via a line search, normalized by the measured Δ​L​(Bmax)Δ𝐿subscript𝐵max\Delta L\left(B_{{\rm max}}\right). Early in training, smaller batches are sufficient to make optimal progress, while larger batches are required later in training.
items.capZh: 图5：左图：对于使用SGD训练的SVHN分类器，展示了不同批量大小下的最优学习率。最优学习率最初随批量大小线性增加，然后按照公式2.7预测的方式趋于平稳。右图：对于一系列批量大小，我们展示了通过线搜索从大小为B的批量中获得的平均损失进展ΔL(B)，归一化为测量的ΔL(B_max)。在训练早期，较小的批量足以实现最优进展，而训练后期需要更大的批量。

items.en: In order to test our model we train each task on a range of batch sizes, selecting the optimal constant learning rate separately for each batch size using a simple grid search. Across a range of tasks, we produce the following results and compare with our model:
items.zh: 为了测试我们的模型，我们在不同批量大小下训练每个任务，通过简单的网格搜索为每个批量大小选择最优的恒定学习率。在一系列任务中，我们产生以下结果并与我们的模型进行比较：

items.en: Optimal learning rates: When optimizing with plain SGD or momentum, we find that the optimal learning rate follows the functional form of Equation 2.6, as shown in Figure 5. For Adam and RMSProp the optimal learning rate initially obeys a power law ϵ​(B)∝Bαproportional-toitalic-ϵ𝐵superscript𝐵𝛼\epsilon\left(B\right)\propto B^{\alpha} with α𝛼\alpha between 0.5 and 1.0 depending on the task, then becomes roughly constant. The scale at which the optimal learning rate stops increasing is generally somewhat smaller than the typical noise scale. (See Appendix E.2 for a potential explanation for this power law behavior.)
items.zh: 最优学习率：当使用普通SGD或动量优化时，我们发现最优学习率遵循方程2.6的函数形式，如图5所示。对于Adam和RMSProp，最优学习率最初服从幂律ϵ(B)∝B^α，其中α在0.5到1.0之间（取决于任务），然后大致保持不变。最优学习率停止增加的规模通常略小于典型噪声尺度。（关于这种幂律行为的潜在解释，请参见附录E.2。）

items.en: Pareto frontiers: For each batch size, we observe the number of optimization steps and total number of data samples needed to achieve various levels of performance. This allows us to visualize the tradeoff between time-efficiency and compute-efficiency as a Pareto frontier (see Figures 6 and 7). We find that Equation 2.11 fits the shape of these tradeoff curves remarkably well in most cases.
items.zh: 帕累托前沿：对于每个批次大小，我们观察到达到不同性能水平所需的优化步数和总数据样本数。这使我们能够将时间效率和计算效率之间的权衡可视化为帕累托前沿（见图6和图7）。我们发现，在大多数情况下，方程2.11对这些权衡曲线的拟合效果非常好。

items.en: Critical batch size (ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}}): We determine the critical batch size over the course of a training run by fitting the Pareto fronts to the functional form of Equation 2.11 (see Figure 7). This quantifies the point at which scaling efficiency begins to drop. In particular, training runs at batch sizes much less than ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} behave similarly per training example, while training runs at batch sizes much larger than ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} behave similarly per optimization step (see Figure 6). The critical batch size typically increases by an order of magnitude or more over the course of training.
items.zh: 临界批次大小（ℬ_crit）：我们通过将帕累托前沿拟合到方程2.11的函数形式（见图7），在训练过程中确定临界批次大小。这量化了扩展效率开始下降的点。特别地，批次大小远小于ℬ_crit的训练运行在每个训练样本上的行为相似，而批次大小远大于ℬ_crit的训练运行在每个优化步骤上的行为相似（见图6）。临界批次大小在训练过程中通常增加一个数量级或更多。

items.en: Simple noise scale (ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}}): We measure the simple noise scale of Equation 2.9 over the course of a single training run using the minimal-overhead procedure described in Appendix A.1. Note that some care must be taken to obtain a proper estimate of ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} due to its dependence on the learning rate via the ‘temperature’ of training. We find that the noise scale agrees between different well-tuned training runs when compared at equal values of the loss, so it can be accurately measured at small batch size (see Appendix C). We also find that, aside from some fluctuations early in training, ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} typically predicts the critical batch size at the order of magnitude level (see Figure 7). The noise scale also typically increases over the course of training, tracking the critical batch size. To obtain a single value for the noise scale representing a full training run, we average over a training run as described in Appendix D.
items.zh: 简单噪声尺度（ℬ_simple）：我们使用附录A.1中描述的最小开销程序，在单次训练运行过程中测量方程2.9的简单噪声尺度。注意，由于简单噪声尺度通过训练的“温度”依赖于学习率，因此需要谨慎以获得正确的估计。我们发现，当在相同的损失值下比较时，不同良好调优的训练运行之间的噪声尺度一致，因此可以在小批次大小下准确测量（见附录C）。我们还发现，除了训练初期的一些波动外，ℬ_simple通常能在数量级水平上预测临界批次大小（见图7）。噪声尺度在训练过程中通常也会增加，与临界批次大小保持一致。为了获得代表整个训练运行的单一噪声尺度值，我们按照附录D中的描述对训练运行进行平均。

items.en: Full noise scale (ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}}): For SVHN trained with SGD, we also measure the full noise scale ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise\mathcal{B}_{{\rm noise}} by performing line searches for gradients obtained by batches of varying size, then fit to the functional form 2.11 (see Figures 5 and 7). This is a somewhat better estimate of ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} but is less computationally convenient, so we choose to focus on ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} for the remaining tasks.
items.zh: 完整噪声尺度（ℬ_noise）：对于使用SGD训练的SVHN，我们还通过使用不同大小的批次获得的梯度进行线搜索，然后拟合到方程2.11的函数形式（见图5和图7），测量了完整噪声尺度ℬ_noise。这是对ℬ_crit的更好估计，但计算上不太方便，因此我们选择在其余任务中专注于ℬ_simple。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Training runs for a simple CNN classifier on the SVHN dataset at constant batch sizes. Small batch training is more compute-efficient (right), while large-batch training requires fewer optimizer steps (left). The turning point between time-efficient and compute-efficient training occurs roughly at B=64𝐵64B=64 for the initial phase of training and increases later in training.
items.capZh: 图6：在SVHN数据集上以恒定批次大小训练简单CNN分类器的运行。小批次训练计算效率更高（右），而大批次训练需要更少的优化器步骤（左）。时间效率和计算效率之间的转折点大致出现在训练初始阶段的B=64，并在训练后期增加。

items.capEn: Figure 7: The tradeoff between time-efficiency and compute-efficiency can be visualized as a Pareto frontier. Each point on the diagram above (left) represents the number of optimizer steps and processed examples needed to achieve a particular level of classification accuracy. Fits to Equation 2.11 are also shown.
items.capZh: 图7：时间效率和计算效率之间的权衡可以可视化为帕累托前沿。上图（左）中的每个点代表达到特定分类准确率所需的优化器步骤数和处理样本数。还显示了方程2.11的拟合结果。

items.en: We summarize our findings in Figure 4: across many tasks, the typical simple noise scale approximately predicts the batch size at which the returns from increasing scale begin to diminish significantly. Results for all of the tasks can be found in Appendix 1. We provide a detailed discussion of our methods in Appendix A.
items.zh: 我们在图4中总结了我们的发现：在许多任务中，典型的简单噪声尺度大致预测了批次大小，超过该大小后，增加规模的收益开始显著减少。所有任务的结果见附录1。我们在附录A中详细讨论了我们的方法。

items.en: Basic image classification results are pictured in Figure 14.
items.zh: 基本的图像分类结果如图14所示。

items.en: SVHN We train a simple CNN image classifier on the extended SVHN dataset [NWC+11]. We display all three of ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}, ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple}, and ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise{\cal B}_{\rm noise} for SVHN optimized with SGD in Figure 7. We find that ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise{\cal B}_{\rm noise} better-predicts ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit} as compared to the more naive ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple}. We compare to training using the Adam optimizer [KB14] in Figure 14, where ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} provides a very accurate prediction for ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}.
items.zh: SVHN：我们在扩展的SVHN数据集[NWC+11]上训练了一个简单的CNN图像分类器。我们在图7中展示了使用SGD优化的SVHN的ℬ_crit、ℬ_simple和ℬ_noise。我们发现，与更朴素的ℬ_simple相比，ℬ_noise能更好地预测ℬ_crit。我们在图14中与使用Adam优化器[KB14]的训练进行了比较，其中ℬ_simple对ℬ_crit提供了非常准确的预测。

items.en: MNIST We train a simple CNN on the MNIST dataset [LC10] using SGD, and find that ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} roughly estimates ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}, though the latter is significantly smaller.
items.zh: MNIST：我们在MNIST数据集[LC10]上使用SGD训练了一个简单的CNN，发现ℬ_simple大致估计了ℬ_crit，尽管后者明显更小。

items.en: CIFAR10 We train a size 32 ResNet [HZRS15] with Momentum on CIFAR10 [Kri09] and find that ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} predicts ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}.
items.zh: CIFAR10：我们在CIFAR10[Kri09]上使用动量训练了一个大小为32的ResNet[HZRS15]，发现ℬ_simple预测了ℬ_crit。

items.en: ImageNet We train a size 50 ResNet [HZRS15] with Momentum on ImageNet [DDS+09], and use a learning rate schedule that decays the learning rate three times during training. Due to the schedule, both ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple}, and ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit} change significantly during training (see Appendix C for a discussion) and must be measured separately at each learning rate. Results are pictured in Figure 10. We find that the noise scale varies from 2,000 to 100,000 in the main phase of training, which matches empirical work (e.g. [JSH+18]) showing that constant batch sizes up to 64 thousand can be used without a loss of efficiency. During the later fine-tuning phase of training, the noise scale increases further to hundreds of thousands and even millions, suggesting that even larger batch sizes may be useful at the very end of training. Our critical batch sizes are slightly lower (15k vs 64k) than those reported in the literature, but we did not use the latest techniques such as layer-wise adaptive learning rates [YGG17].
items.zh: ImageNet：我们在ImageNet[DDS+09]上使用动量训练了一个大小为50的ResNet[HZRS15]，并使用在训练过程中衰减三次学习率的学习率调度。由于调度，ℬ_simple和ℬ_crit在训练过程中都发生了显著变化（讨论见附录C），必须在每个学习率下分别测量。结果如图10所示。我们发现，在训练的主要阶段，噪声尺度从2,000变化到100,000，这与实证工作（例如[JSH+18]）一致，表明可以使用高达64,000的恒定批次大小而不会损失效率。在训练后期的微调阶段，噪声尺度进一步增加到数十万甚至数百万，表明在训练结束时使用更大的批次大小可能是有用的。我们的临界批次大小略低于文献中报告的值（15k vs 64k），但我们没有使用最新的技术，如逐层自适应学习率[YGG17]。

items.en: Overall we find that more complex image datasets have larger noise scales in a way that is not directly determined by dataset size.
items.zh: 总体而言，我们发现更复杂的图像数据集具有更大的噪声尺度，且这种关系并非直接由数据集大小决定。

items.en: The results for these tasks are pictured in Figure 9.
items.zh: 这些任务的结果如图9所示。

items.en: VAE and Autoencoder We train a VAE [KW13] and a simple Autoencoder on the SVHN dataset [NWC+11]; we were motivated to compare these models because VAEs introduce additional stochasticity. As expected, the VAE had larger ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit} and ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} as compared to the Autoencoder, and both models had much lower ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} as compared to SVHN image classifiers. However, unlike most of the other tasks, for these generative models ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple} was significantly smaller than ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}.
items.zh: VAE和自编码器：我们在SVHN数据集[NWC+11]上训练了一个VAE[KW13]和一个简单的自编码器；我们比较这些模型的动机是VAE引入了额外的随机性。正如预期，与自编码器相比，VAE具有更大的ℬ_crit和ℬ_simple，并且两个模型的ℬ_simple都远低于SVHN图像分类器。然而，与大多数其他任务不同，对于这些生成模型，ℬ_simple明显小于ℬ_crit。

items.en: Language Modeling We train a single-layer LSTM for autoregressive prediction on the Billion Word dataset [CMS+13], and find good agreement between ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit} and ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{\rm simple}. We also illustrate the dependence on LSTM size in Figure 8, finding that the noise scale is roughly independent of LSTM size at fixed values of the loss, but that larger LSTMs eventually achieve lower values of the loss and a larger noise scale.
items.zh: 语言建模：我们在Billion Word数据集[CMS+13]上训练了一个单层LSTM进行自回归预测，发现ℬ_crit和ℬ_simple之间具有良好的一致性。我们还在图8中展示了LSTM大小的依赖性，发现在固定损失值下，噪声尺度大致与LSTM大小无关，但更大的LSTM最终会达到更低的损失值和更大的噪声尺度。

items.en: Atari We train RL agents with the policy gradient algorithm A2C [MBM+16] on seven Atari games [BNVB12] (Alien, Beamrider, Breakout, Pong, Qbert, Seaquest, Space Invaders), with results pictured in Figures 11 and 12. The tradeoff curves generally agree well with the prediction of Equation 2.11, though they are somewhat noisy e.g. for Pong since we do not average over multiple seeds. For some Atari games, we find some consistent deviation from 12 at very small batch sizes (see e.g. Beam Rider in Figure 11). It would be interesting to study this phenomenon further, though this could simply indicate greater sensitivity to other hyperparameters (e.g. momentum) at small batch size. Overall, we see that patterns in the noise scale match intuition, such as Pong being much easier to learn than other Atari games.
items.zh: Atari：我们在七个Atari游戏[BNVB12]（Alien, Beamrider, Breakout, Pong, Qbert, Seaquest, Space Invaders）上使用策略梯度算法A2C[MBM+16]训练了强化学习智能体，结果如图11和图12所示。权衡曲线通常与方程2.11的预测吻合良好，尽管有些噪声，例如Pong，因为我们没有对多个种子进行平均。对于某些Atari游戏，我们发现在非常小的批次大小下存在一些一致的偏差（例如图11中的Beam Rider）。进一步研究这一现象将是有趣的，尽管这可能仅仅表明在小的批次大小下对其他超参数（例如动量）更敏感。总体而言，我们看到噪声尺度的模式与直觉相符，例如Pong比其他Atari游戏更容易学习。

items.en: Dota The OpenAI Dota team has made it possible to train PPO [SWD+17] agents on both Dota 1v1 and 5v5 environments (the latter being preliminary ongoing work). We vary the two hyperparameters batch size and learning rate on the existing code, experiment setup, and training infrastructure as described in [BCD+18]. The Dota 1v1 environment features two agents fighting in a restricted part of the map (although they are free to walk anywhere) with a fixed set of abilities and skills, whereas Dota 5v5 involves the whole map, 5 heroes on each side, and vastly more configurations in which heroes might engage each other. This is reflected in the higher noise scale for Dota 5v5 (at least 10 million) relative to Dota 1v1 – we suspect the higher diversity of situations gives rise to more variance in the gradients. Due to resource constraints we were not able to measure the Pareto fronts for Dota 5v5, and so we can only report the batch size used by the Dota team and the measured noise scale.
items.zh: Dota：OpenAI Dota团队使得在Dota 1v1和5v5环境（后者是初步的进行中工作）上训练PPO[SWD+17]智能体成为可能。我们在现有代码、实验设置和训练基础设施上变化两个超参数——批次大小和学习率，如[BCD+18]所述。Dota 1v1环境涉及两个智能体在地图的受限部分（尽管它们可以自由行走）战斗，具有固定的能力和技能集，而Dota 5v5涉及整个地图，每方5个英雄，以及英雄之间可能交战的更多配置。这反映在Dota 5v5（至少1000万）相对于Dota 1v1更高的噪声尺度上——我们怀疑更高的情境多样性导致了梯度中更大的方差。由于资源限制，我们无法测量Dota 5v5的帕累托前沿，因此我们只能报告Dota团队使用的批次大小和测量的噪声尺度。

items.en: Results for the tasks described above were generally within a reasonable margin of state-of-the-art results, though we did not explicitly try to match SOTA or use special algorithmic or architectural tricks. Our goal was simply to confirm that we were in a reasonably well-performing regime that is typical of ML practice.
items.zh: 上述任务的结果通常与最先进的结果相差不大，尽管我们没有明确尝试匹配SOTA或使用特殊的算法或架构技巧。我们的目标仅仅是确认我们处于一个性能合理的典型机器学习实践范围内。

items.en: For the supervised learning and generative modeling tasks listed above, we have the option of using either training set or test set performance to compare different batch sizes. For the main results in this paper, we choose train set performance because it is what is directly predicted by our model, and because it is easier to measure in the presence of overfitting. The choice makes negligible difference in most of our experiments, either because they involve RL or because the datasets are large enough that we don’t overfit. On the small datasets MNIST, CIFAR10, and SVHN, overfitting makes measurement of test error more difficult, but we do measure the test error behavior in Appendix E.3, and both the Pareto fronts and critical batch size generally do not change much.
items.zh: 对于上述监督学习和生成建模任务，我们可以选择使用训练集或测试集性能来比较不同批次大小。在本文的主要结果中，我们选择训练集性能，因为它直接由我们的模型预测，并且在存在过拟合时更容易测量。这一选择在我们的大多数实验中影响可忽略不计，要么是因为它们涉及强化学习，要么是因为数据集足够大以至于我们不会过拟合。在小型数据集MNIST、CIFAR10和SVHN上，过拟合使得测试误差的测量更加困难，但我们在附录E.3中确实测量了测试误差行为，并且帕累托前沿和临界批次大小通常变化不大。

items.en: The fact that the noise scale is consistent between well-tuned training runs suggests that the corresponding optimization trajectories are similar in some sense. In Appendix C we investigate this idea further and relate the noise scale to a characteristic ‘temperature’ of the training process.
items.zh: 噪声尺度在调优良好的训练运行之间保持一致这一事实表明，相应的优化轨迹在某种意义上是相似的。在附录C中，我们进一步探讨了这一想法，并将噪声尺度与训练过程的特征“温度”联系起来。

items.capEn: Figure 8: We show the relationship between training perplexity and the simple noise scale (left) for a range of LSTM sizes on the Billion Word dataset. These results show that at fixed values of the loss, the noise scale does not depend significantly on model size. On the right we show the simple noise scale during training, plotted in terms of the number of tokens processed. After processing a given number of examples, larger models will tend to have a larger noise scale, but only as a consequence of having achieved smaller loss.
items.capZh: 图8：我们展示了在Billion Word数据集上，一系列LSTM大小的训练困惑度与简单噪声尺度（左图）之间的关系。这些结果表明，在损失值固定的情况下，噪声尺度并不显著依赖于模型大小。右图显示了训练过程中的简单噪声尺度，以处理的token数量为横轴。在处理给定数量的样本后，较大的模型往往具有较大的噪声尺度，但这只是由于它们实现了更小的损失。

items.en: The definitions of the noise scales do not have any manifest dependence on the number of parameters in a model. We have conjectured that they will be roughly independent of model size at fixed values of the loss.
items.zh: 噪声尺度的定义并不显式依赖于模型中的参数数量。我们推测，在损失值固定的情况下，它们将大致独立于模型大小。

items.en: LSTM language models provide a natural test case, since LSTM sizes can be scaled up without qualitatively altering model architecture. As shown in Figure 8, the simple noise scale appears to be roughly independent of model size at a fixed value of the loss. However, due to their higher performance and lower achieved loss, larger models eventually reach larger noise scales than smaller models. We do not have specific hypotheses for how the noise scale should vary with model architecture, but interesting results along these lines were recently obtained [SLA+18].
items.zh: LSTM语言模型提供了一个自然的测试案例，因为LSTM的大小可以缩放而不改变模型架构。如图8所示，在损失值固定的情况下，简单噪声尺度似乎大致独立于模型大小。然而，由于较大的模型性能更高且损失更低，它们最终会达到比小模型更大的噪声尺度。我们对于噪声尺度如何随模型架构变化没有具体的假设，但最近沿着这些方向获得了有趣的结果[SLA+18]。

items.en: A great deal of prior work has studied large-batch training, investigated versions of the noise scale, explored adaptive batch size and learning rate schedules, and demonstrated that large batch training can be effective on specific datasets. We attempt to summarize this work below.
items.zh: 大量先前的工作研究了大批量训练，探讨了噪声尺度的各种版本，探索了自适应批次大小和学习率调度，并证明了大批量训练在特定数据集上的有效性。我们试图在下面总结这些工作。

items.en: Recent papers have probed the limits of large batch training empirically, especially for ImageNet [GDG+17, YZH+17, JSH+18], in some cases using layer-wise adaptive learning-rates [YGG17]. More recent work has demonstrated that large batch training can also be applied to RL [AAG+18, BCD+18, SA18, HQB+18]. The use of second order optimization methods [BGM17] might increase the utility of data parallelism even further. A thorough review of large batch training and potential issues with generalization was provided in a very nice recent empirical study [SLA+18] done in parallel with this work. [GVY+18] also systematically studied large-batch training, though it did not tune the learning rate separately for each batch size.
items.zh: 最近的论文从经验上探索了大批量训练的极限，特别是对于ImageNet [GDG+17, YZH+17, JSH+18]，在某些情况下使用了逐层自适应学习率[YGG17]。更近的工作表明，大批量训练也可以应用于强化学习[AAG+18, BCD+18, SA18, HQB+18]。使用二阶优化方法[BGM17]可能会进一步提高数据并行的效用。一项与本文同时进行的非常优秀的实证研究[SLA+18]对大批量训练及其泛化潜在问题进行了全面回顾。[GVY+18]也系统地研究了大批量训练，尽管它没有为每个批次大小单独调整学习率。

items.en: Other recent work has explored the impact of gradient noise on optimization speed and batch size selection. [SZL13] connected gradient noise and the locally optimal step size to identify an adaptive learning rate. [MHB17] derived a sampling distribution for SGD, motivating our definition of ‘temperature’. [SL17] connected this temperature to the critical batch size, though they predict a dependence on dataset size which we do not observe. [SZT17] identified a signal-dominated and noise-dominated phase of training. [SKYL17] showed that decaying the learning rate and increasing the batch size have the same effect, motivated by the SGD training temperature. ([DNG17] also suggested increasing learning rate and batch size together, but with different motivation.) [IES+18] empirically investigated the role of gradient noise in reinforcement learning.
items.zh: 其他近期工作探讨了梯度噪声对优化速度和批次大小选择的影响。[SZL13]将梯度噪声与局部最优步长联系起来，以确定自适应学习率。[MHB17]推导了SGD的采样分布，激发了我们定义“温度”。[SL17]将这个温度与临界批次大小联系起来，尽管他们预测了数据集大小的依赖性，但我们并未观察到。[SZT17]识别了训练的信号主导和噪声主导阶段。[SKYL17]表明，衰减学习率和增加批次大小具有相同的效果，这是由SGD训练温度驱动的。（[DNG17]也建议同时增加学习率和批次大小，但动机不同。）[IES+18]从经验上研究了梯度噪声在强化学习中的作用。

items.en: The gradient noise scale in particular has also been studied in earlier work to aid in batch size selection. The noise scale itself is used implicitly in basic statistical techniques for sample size selection (see e.g. [Wik, NIS]). [BCNW12] implicitly uses the gradient noise scale for a theoretical analysis of batch size selection. [BCN16, DYJG16, BRH16] propose adaptive sampling methods based on the gradient noise scale in the context of neural network optimization. [YPL+17] analyzed the gradient noise scale for a particular class of functions and related it to the critical batch size, though it predicts a sharp change in learning speed with batch size rather than the smooth change we observe. [CWZ+18] theoretically analyzed the dependence of the gradient noise scale on network width for shallow or linear networks, though they find inconsistent empirical results on neural networks. [MBB17] found a formula for the optimization speedup in terms of batch size resembling ours, though their critical batch size depends on smoothness parameters of the loss rather than directly gradient noise.
items.zh: 梯度噪声尺度在早期工作中也被研究以帮助选择批次大小。噪声尺度本身隐式地用于基本的样本量选择统计技术（例如，参见[Wik, NIS]）。[BCNW12]隐式地使用梯度噪声尺度进行批次大小选择的理论分析。[BCN16, DYJG16, BRH16]在神经网络优化背景下提出了基于梯度噪声尺度的自适应采样方法。[YPL+17]针对一类特定函数分析了梯度噪声尺度，并将其与临界批次大小联系起来，尽管它预测了学习速度随批次大小的急剧变化，而不是我们观察到的平滑变化。[CWZ+18]从理论上分析了浅层或线性网络中梯度噪声尺度对网络宽度的依赖性，尽管他们在神经网络上发现了不一致的经验结果。[MBB17]找到了一个类似于我们的批次大小优化加速公式，尽管他们的临界批次大小取决于损失的平滑性参数，而不是直接取决于梯度噪声。

items.en: There has been a variety of work studying the Neural Network loss landscape and using it to draw conclusions about optimal training. Local properties of the loss landscape are not necessarily a good guide to overall optimal training [WRLG18]. The loss tends to be fairly smooth when interpolating between the start and end of training [GVS14]. But noise may be useful early in training [NVL+15, YPL+17], perhaps because it leads to minima that generalize better [KMN+16].
items.zh: 已有多种工作研究神经网络损失景观，并利用它得出关于最优训练的结论。损失景观的局部性质不一定是整体最优训练的良好指南[WRLG18]。在训练开始和结束之间插值时，损失往往相当平滑[GVS14]。但噪声可能在训练早期有用[NVL+15, YPL+17]，也许是因为它导致泛化更好的最小值[KMN+16]。

items.en: A big-picture motivation for our work was to better understand the scaling of learning with computational and data resources; this question was addressed from the perspective of scaling the model size in [HNA+17].
items.zh: 我们工作的一个宏观动机是更好地理解学习随计算和数据资源的缩放；这个问题在[HNA+17]中从模型大小缩放的角度得到了解决。

items.en: Our key contributions include connecting the gradient noise scale to the speed of optimization with a simple model, as well as systematically measuring the critical batch size and noise scale for a variety of tasks. We also clarify the role of the training temperature in SGD and propose an optimal batch size schedule.
items.zh: 我们的关键贡献包括：通过一个简单模型将梯度噪声尺度与优化速度联系起来，以及系统地测量各种任务的临界批次大小和噪声尺度。我们还阐明了训练温度在SGD中的作用，并提出了一个最优批次大小调度。

items.en: We have shown that the simplified gradient noise scale ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} approximately predicts the actual point of diminishing return on batch size ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit} on diverse problems where these quantities vary by six orders of magnitude. Furthermore, the tradeoff curve between total compute and optimization steps associated with changing the batch size has roughly the hyperbolic form predicted by our theory. Finally, our theory also roughly predicts how the optimal learning rate scales with batch size, although its predictions are not as precise.
items.zh: 我们已经证明，简化的梯度噪声尺度ℬsimple大致预测了实际批次大小收益递减点ℬcrit，在多样的问题上这些量变化了六个数量级。此外，与改变批次大小相关的总计算量和优化步骤之间的权衡曲线大致具有我们理论预测的双曲线形式。最后，我们的理论也大致预测了最优学习率如何随批次大小缩放，尽管其预测并不精确。

items.en: What does the validity of this theory mean, and in what way is it useful? At the level of a given task, it allows us to use the noise scale from a single run (even an only partially complete run with much smaller batch size, though see caveats about learning rate tuning in the appendix) to estimate the largest useful batch size, and thus reduces the extensive hyperparameter searches that are necessary to find this batch size by trial and error. It also tells us to expect that larger batch sizes will show diminishing returns in a predictable way that has the same form regardless of the task.
items.zh: 这个理论的有效性意味着什么，它有什么用处？在给定任务层面，它允许我们使用单次运行（甚至只是批次大小小得多的部分完成运行，但关于学习率调优的注意事项见附录）中的噪声尺度来估计最大有用批次大小，从而减少了通过试错找到这个批次大小所需的大量超参数搜索。它还告诉我们，更大的批次大小将以可预测的方式显示出收益递减，且无论任务如何，这种形式都是相同的。

items.en: Across tasks, it tells us that the largest useful batch size for a task is likely to be correlated to informal notions of the “complexity” of the task, because the noise scale essentially measures how diverse the data is (as seen by the model), which is one aspect of task complexity.
items.zh: 跨任务而言，它告诉我们，任务的最大有用批次大小很可能与任务“复杂性”的非正式概念相关，因为噪声尺度本质上衡量了数据（从模型角度看）的多样性，这是任务复杂性的一个方面。

items.en: We have argued that a specific formula characterizes the time/compute tradeoff between optimization steps and total data processed in neural network training:
items.zh: 我们已经论证，一个特定的公式刻画了神经网络训练中优化步骤与总处理数据之间的时间/计算权衡：

items.tex: \displaystyle\left(\frac{{\rm Optimization\ Steps}}{{\rm Min\ Steps}}-1\right)\left(\frac{{\rm Data\ Examples}}{{\rm Min\ Examples}}-1\right)

items.en: From this relation we can identify a critical value of the batch size when training to a given value of the loss
items.zh: 从这个关系，我们可以识别出训练到给定损失值时批次大小的临界值

items.en: Training at this critical batch size provides a natural compromise between time and compute, as we take only twice the minimum number of optimization steps and use only twice the minimum amount of data. The critical batch size represents a turning point, so that for B>ℬcrit𝐵subscriptℬcritB>{\cal B}_{\rm crit} there are diminishing returns from greater data parallelism.
items.zh: 以这个临界批次大小训练提供了时间和计算之间的自然折中，因为我们只用了最小优化步骤数的两倍和最小数据量的两倍。临界批次大小代表一个转折点，因此对于B>ℬcrit，更大的数据并行性带来的收益递减。

items.en: Our main goal was to provide a simple way to predict ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{\rm crit}. We have shown that it can be estimated as
items.zh: 我们的主要目标是提供一种简单的方法来预测ℬcrit。我们已经证明它可以估计为

items.en: where the easily-measured ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple\mathcal{B}_{{\rm simple}} is the ratio of the gradient variance to its squared mean. Theoretical arguments suggest that a more refined quantity, the Hessian-weighted ℬnoisesubscriptℬnoise{\cal B}_{{\rm noise}} of Equation 2.8, may provide an even better888We have also investigated using gradients preconditioned by the Adam optimizer; the results were mixed. estimate of ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}}.
items.zh: 其中容易测量的ℬsimple是梯度方差与其平方均值的比值。理论论证表明，一个更精细的量，即方程2.8中的Hessian加权ℬnoise，可能提供对ℬcrit更好的估计888我们也研究了使用Adam优化器预处理的梯度；结果好坏参半。

items.en: The tradeoff curve of Equation 5.1 provides a remarkably good fit across datasets, models, and optimizers, and the approximate equality of ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} and ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}} holds even as both quantities vary greatly between tasks and training regimes. We have established that as anticipated, both ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} and ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}} tend to increase significantly during training, that they are larger for more complex tasks, and that they are roughly independent of model size (for LSTMs) at fixed values of the loss. We also saw that image classification has a significantly larger per-image noise scale as compared to generative models training on the same dataset, a fact that could have interesting implications for model-based RL. In the case of RL, while the noise scale for Dota was roughly a thousand times larger than that of Atari, the total number of optimization steps needed to train a Dota agent is not so much larger [BCD+18]. Perhaps this suggests that much of the additional compute needed to train more powerful models will be parallelizable.
items.zh: 方程5.1的权衡曲线在数据集、模型和优化器之间提供了极好的拟合，并且ℬcrit和ℬsimple的近似相等性成立，即使这两个量在不同任务和训练机制之间变化很大。我们已经确定，正如预期的那样，ℬcrit和ℬsimple在训练过程中都显著增加，对于更复杂的任务它们更大，并且在损失固定时大致与模型大小（对于LSTM）无关。我们还看到，与在同一数据集上训练的生成模型相比，图像分类具有显著更大的每图像噪声尺度，这一事实可能对基于模型的强化学习产生有趣的影响。在强化学习的情况下，虽然Dota的噪声尺度大约是Atari的一千倍，但训练Dota智能体所需的总优化步骤数并没有大那么多[BCD+18]。也许这表明训练更强大模型所需的额外算力大部分将是可并行的。

items.en: While ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}} roughly matches ℬcritsubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} for all datasets, the ratio ℬsimple/ℬcritsubscriptℬsimplesubscriptℬcrit{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}}/{\cal B}_{{\rm crit}} can vary by about an order of magnitude between tasks. This may not be so surprising, since ℬsimplesubscriptℬsimple{\cal B}_{{\rm simple}} does not take into account Hessian conditioning or global aspects of the loss landscape. But it would be very interesting to obtain a better understanding of this ratio. It was smaller than one for the Autoencoder, VAE, and for Dota 1v1, roughly equal to one for LSTMs, and greater than one for both image classification tasks and Atari, and we lack an explanation for these variations. It would certainly be interesting to study this ratio in other classes of models, and to further explore the behavior of generative models.
items.zh: 虽然ℬsimple在所有数据集上大致匹配ℬcrit，但ℬsimple/ℬcrit的比值在不同任务之间可能变化约一个数量级。这可能并不令人惊讶，因为ℬsimple没有考虑Hessian条件或损失景观的全局方面。但更好地理解这个比值将非常有趣。对于自编码器、VAE和Dota 1v1，它小于1；对于LSTM，大致等于1；对于图像分类任务和Atari，大于1，我们缺乏对这些变化的解释。研究其他模型类别中的这个比值，并进一步探索生成模型的行为，当然会很有趣。

items.en: Due to its crucial role in data-parallelism, we have focused on the batch size B𝐵B, presuming that the learning rate or effective ‘temperature’ will be optimized after B𝐵B has been chosen. And our theoretical treatment focused on a specific point in the loss landscape, ignoring issues such as the relationship between early and late training and the necessity of a ‘warm-up’ period. It would be interesting to address these issues, particularly insofar as they may provide motivation for adaptive batch sizes.
items.zh: 由于其在数据并行中的关键作用，我们专注于批量大小B，假设学习率或有效“温度”将在选择B后进行优化。我们的理论处理集中在损失景观中的一个特定点，忽略了诸如早期和晚期训练之间的关系以及“预热”阶段的必要性等问题。解决这些问题将是有趣的，特别是因为它们可能为自适应批量大小提供动机。
